hadiths.ar: 202  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّس َّو َّهِي ن َّ ق ِى  ج َّلْق ِهِمْ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
'Whoever has intercourse with his wife while She is menstruating, and if the child born 
from that union is afflicted with leprosy or vitiligo, he should blame only himself.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 212 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 
3, p. 307 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 22, p. 719 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 318 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 201
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠١

hadiths.ar:
203 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «. 
وي سي ن زى  إلأ مه  إ ذ إ إس ي زي ث  ن حي ص ه ، ومن  إس ي زى إ مه  ق ذج ل ن ها ق ن ل إ ن  ي سي ن زإ ها ق ق ذ ر ن ى ن ماله  وإ ذ إ إ رإذب  إلمزإ ة  إلع سل 
من  إلحي ض  ق علي ها إن  ي سي ن زى ، وإلأسي ن زإء إ ن  ي ذج ل ق طن ه  ق ا ن  كان  هن اك  ذم ج زح  ولو مي ل رإ س إلذ ن اب  ق ا ن  ج زح  لم  
ي ع ي سل، وإ ن  لم ي ح زح  إع ي سلث ، وإ ذ إ رإ ب  إلصق زة  وإلن ي ن  ق علي ها إ ن  ي لصق  ن طن ها ن الحان ظ وي زق غ رج لها إلي سزى كما ي زى 
إلكلث  إ ذ إ ن ال وي ذج ل ق طن ه   ق ا ن  ج زح  ق ي ها ذم ق هى  جان ض ، وإ ن  لم ي ح زح  ق لي س ن حان ض .  وإ ن  إس ي ن ه علي ها ذم إلحي ض  وذم 
إلق زجه ، ق زن ما كان  ق ى  ق زج ها ق زجه  ق علي ها إ ن  ي سي لق ى  على ق ق ان ها وي ذج ل إ صن عها ق ا ن  ج زح  إلذم من  إل ح ان ث  إلأ ي من  ق هو 
من  إلق زجه ، وإ ن  ج زح  من  إلح ان ث  إلأ ي سز ق هو من  إلحي ض  . وإن  إق ي ص ها ر وج ها ولم ي زق ا  ذمها ولأ ي ذرى  ذم إلحي ض  هو إ م 
ذم إلعذ رة  ؟  ق علي ها ي ذج ل ق طن ه ، ق ا ن  ج زج ث  إلق طن ه  مطوق ه  ن الذم ق هو من  إلعذ رة ، وإ ن  ج زج ث  من ع مسه  ق هو من  إلحي ض .  
وذم إلعذ رة  لأ ي ح ور  إلس ق زي ن  وذم إلحي ض  جار ي ح زح  ن حزإرة  س ذي ذة .  وذم إلمسي حاص ه  ن ارذ ي سي ل من ها وهى  لأ ي علم، كذ لك  
ذ كزة إ ن ى    رجمه إلله  ق ى  رسالي ه إ لى .  ق ا ذ إ رإ ب  إلذم ج مسه  إ ي ام وإلطهز ج مسه  إ ي ام إ و رإ ب  إلذم إ رن عه  إ ي ام وإلطهز سي ه  إ ي ام،  
ق ا ذ إ رإ ب  إلذم لم ي صل، وإ ذ إ رإ ب  إلطهز صلث ، ي ق عل ذ لك  ما ن ي ن ها ون ي ن  ي  لاي ي ن  ي وما، ق ا ذ إ مص ث  ي لاي ون  ي وما ي م رإ ب  ذما 
صن ي ن ا  إع ي سلث  وإجي س ث  ن الكزسف  وإسي ي ق زب  ق ى  وق ث  كل صلاة ، وإ ذ إ رإ ب  صق زة  ي وص ا ب  . وإلمزإ ة  إلحان ض  إ ذ إ رإ ب  إلطهز 
ق ى  إلسق ز  ولي س معها ماء ي كق ي ها لع سلها وجص زب  إلصلاة  ق ا ن  كان  معها من  إلماء ق ذر ما ي ع سل ن ه ق زج ها ع سلي ه وي ي ممث  
وصلث ، وجل لز وج ها إ ن  ي ا ي ي ها ق ى  ي لك  إلحال إ ذ إ ع سلث  ق زج ها وي ي ممث ، ولأ ي ح ور  للن ساء إ ن  ي ن ط زن  إ لى إ ن ق سهن  ق ى  
إلمحي ض  لأ ن هن  ق ذ ن هي ن  عن  ذ لك  .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about those who are 
deformed in their creation.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'They are those whose fathers engaged in intercourse with their wives 
during menstruation.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 202

hadiths.ar:
204   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عُّن َّي ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّان ِض ِ مَّا ي َّحِل ُّ لِز َّوْج ِهَّا مِن ْهَّا ق َّال 
ن ِا ِر َّإرٍ إ ِلَّى إَّلز ُّكْن َّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ ي ُّح ْزِح ُّ سُّز َّي َّهَّا ي ُّم َّ لَّهُّ مَّا ق َّوْق َّ إَّلْأ ِر َّإرِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
'No one harbors hatred toward us except one whose birth was impure or whose 
mother conceived him while She was menstruating.' 
[AL SADUQ] 
A slave woman must observe a waiting period (istibra ʾ) of one menstrual cycle upon 
being purchased, and if someone purchases a slave and has intercourse with her 
before this waiting period, he has committed fornication with his own property.  
If a woman wishes to perform ghusl (ritual bathing) after menstruation, She must 
ensure She is free of menstrual blood (istibraʾ). This means inserting a piece of cotton; 
if blood is present, even the size of a fly's head, She should not perform ghusl. If no 
blood is present, She may perform ghusl. If She notices a yellowish or foul -smelling 
discharge, She should press her abdomen against a wall and raise her left leg, similar 
to the way a dog urinates, and insert a cotton piece. If it comes out with blood, She is 
still menstruating; if not, She is not. 
If She is unsure whether the blood is menstrual or from a wound in her private area, 
She should lie on her back and insert her finger; if the blood comes from the right side, 
it is from the wound, and if it comes from the left side, it is menstrual blood.  
If her virginity was broken by her husband and bleeding does not stop, and She does 
not know whether it is menstrual blood or blood from the loss of virginity, She should 
insert a cotton piece; if it comes out encircled with blood, it is from the rupture, and if 
it is soaked, it is menstrual blood. Blood from the rupture does not exceed the labia, 
but menstrual blood is warm and flows with intensity. Blood of irregular bleed 
(istihadah) is cold and flows without the woman being aware.  
If a woman sees blood for five days and is pure for five days or sees blood for four days 
and is pure for six days, then during the days She sees blood, She should not pray, and 
when She is pure, She should pray, continuing this cycle for up to thirty days . If thirty 
days pass and She sees a continuous flow of blood, She should perform ghusl, use 
cotton, and secure herself for each prayer time. If She notices yellowish discharge, 
She should perform wudu. 
If a menstruating woman becomes pure while traveling and does not have enough 
water for a full ghusl but has enough to wash her private parts, She should wash it, 
perform dry ablution (tayammum), and pray. It is permissible for her husband to 
approach her in this state after She washes her private parts and performs 
tayammum. Women are discouraged from observing themselves during 
menstruation, as they have been prohibited from doing so.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 22, p. 719 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 318 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 203
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٣

hadiths.ar:
205   -  وَّ ذ َّكَّزَّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّ مَّي ْمُّون َّه َّ كَّان َّث ْ ي َّق ُّولُّ: إ ِن  َّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ و إ لِهِ كَّان َّ ي َّا ْمُّزُّن ِى  إ ِذ َّإ كُّن ْث ُّ  
جَّان ِص اً إ َّن ْ إ َّي  َّز ِرَّ ن ِي َّوْب ٍ ي ُّم َّ إ َّص ْطَّح ِغَّ مَّعَّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِزَّإس ِ .
hadiths.en:
Ubaidullah ibn Ali Al -Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
about what is permissible for a husband with regard to his menstruating wife.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'She should cover herself with a cloth (izar) from her navel to her knees, 
and he may have what is above the izar.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 154 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
129 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 22, p. 737 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 204
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٤

hadiths.en:
It was reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} father, Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, that Maymunah used to say:  
‘The Prophet would instruct me, when I was menstruating, to wrap myself with a cloth 
(izar) and then lie down with him in bed.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 22, p. 737 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 205
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٥

hadiths.ar:
207   -  وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ن ِسْوَّة   مِن ْ ن ِطَّان َّي ِهَّا هَّلْ كَّان َّ جَّي ْص ُّهَّا ق ِي مَّا مَّص َّى عَّلَّى مَّا إِذ َّعَّث ْ ق َّا ِن ْ س َّهِذْن َّ صُّذِّق َّث ْ وَّ إ ِلأ َّ ق َّهِى َّ ك اذ ِن َّه   .
hadiths.en:
(From Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}), He said:  
'The wives of the Prophet did not make up the prayers when they were menstruating. 
However, when the time for prayer entered, they would use cotton (for cleanliness), 
perform wudu (ablution), and then sit near the mosque to remember Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Majestic.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 486 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 345 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 206
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٦

hadiths.ar:
208  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّم َّارُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّان ِض ِ ي َّع ْي َّسِلُّ وَّ عَّلَّى ج َّسَّذِهَّا إَّلز  َّعْق َّزَّإن ُّ 
لَّمْ ي َّذ ْهَّث ْ ن ِهِ إَّلْمَّاءُّ ق َّال   ي َّن ْق ُّض ْ س َّعْزَّهَّا كَّمْ ي ُّح ْز ِي هَّا  
مِن َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ ق َّال ل َّذ ِى  ي َّس ْزَّب ُّ س َّعْزُّهَّا وَّ هُّوَّ ي َّلاَّب ُّ جَّق َّن َّاب ٍ عَّلَّى رَّإ ْسِهَّا وَّ جَّق ْن َّي َّان ِ عَّلَّى إَّلْي َّمِي ن ِ وَّ جَّق ْن َّي َّان ِ عَّلَّى  
إَّلْي َّسَّارِ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّمِز ُّ ي َّذَّهَّا عَّلَّى ج َّسَّذِهَّا كُّلِّهِ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said regarding a 
woman who claimed to have experienced three menstrual cycles in one month:  
'Women from her close circle (those familiar with her cycle) should be asked whether 
her previous menstruation followed the pattern She claims. If they testify in her favour, 
She is to be believed; otherwise, She is considered lying.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 398 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Vol. 6, p. 271 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 8, p. 166 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 148 • Al-Istibsar, Part 3, p. 356 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 23, p. 1261 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 358 • Wasa’il Al -Shia, vol. 27, p. 361 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, 
Vol. 1, p. 473 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 207
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٧

hadiths.ar: 209 - وَّ كَّان َّ ن َّعْض ُّ ن ِسَّاءِ إَّلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ي ُّزَّج ِّلُّ س َّعْزَّهَّا وَّ ي َّع ْسِلُّ رَّإ ْسَّهَّا وَّ هِى َّ جَّان ِ ض  .
hadiths.en:
Ammar ibn Musa al -Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
about a menstruating woman who performs ghusl (ritual bathing) while saffron 
remains on her body and is not removed by the water.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'There is no harm in it.'  
He also asked about a woman who performs ghusl and has braided hair but does not 
undo it; how much water suffices for her?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'The amount that is absorbed by her hair, which is three handfuls over 
her head, two handfuls on the right side, and two handfuls on the left side, after which 
She should run her hands over her entire body.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 208
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٨

hadiths.ar:
الن ق اس وا خكامة  
وإ ذ إ ولذب  إلمزإ ة  ق عذب  عن  إلصلاة  عس زة  إ ي ام إ لأ إ ن  ي طهز ق ن ل ذ لك  ق ان  إسي مز ن ها إلذم ي زكث  إلصلاة  ما ن ي ن ها ون ي ن  ي مان ي ه 
عس ز ي وما، لأن  إ سماء ن ن ث  عمي س ن ق سث  ن محمذ ن ن  إ ن ى  ن كز ق ى  جح ه   إلوذإع ق ا مزها رسول إلله صلى إلله علي ه وإ له إ ن  
ي ق عذ ي مان ي ه  عس ز ي وما .   
210 - وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى ي ْض ِ ي َّلاَّي َّه ُّ إ َّي  َّامٍ وَّ إ َّكْي َّزَّةُّ 
عَّس َّزَّة ُّ إ َّي  َّامٍ ق َّا َّوْسَّطُّهُّ ج َّمْسَّه ُّ إ َّي  َّامٍ ق َّح َّعَّلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ لِلن  ُّق َّسَّا ءِ إ َّي  َّامَّ إ َّق َّلِّ إَّلْحَّي ْض ِ وَّ إ َّوْسَّطِهِ وَّ إ َّكْي َّزِةِ  
وإلأج ن ار إلي ى  روي ث  ق ى  ق عوذها إ رن عي ن  ي وما وما ر إذ إ لى إ ن  ي طهز معلوله  كلها وورذب  للي ق ي ه  لأ ي ق ي ى  ن ها إ لأ إ هل إلح لاف .
hadiths.en:
Some of the wives of the Prophet {saws} used to comb their hair and wash their 
heads while they were menstruating.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 489 
POSTNATAL BLEEDING AND ITS RULINGS  
[The title is an addition from us and not in the original] 
֎ HADITH 210 – 211 ֍ 
[AL SADUQ] 
If a woman gives birth, She should refrain from prayer for ten days, unless She 
becomes pure (stops bleeding) before that.  
If the bleeding continues, She should refrain from prayer for up to eighteen days, 
because Asma bint Umays experienced postnatal bleeding (nifas) with Muhammad 
ibn Abi Bakr during the Farewell Pilgrimage, and the Messenger of Allah {saws} instructed 
her to refrain for eighteen days. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 209
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢٠٩

hadiths.ar:
211   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّم َّارُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إِمْزَّإ ة ٍ إ َّصَّان َّهَّا إَّلط َّلْق ُّ إَّلْي َّوْمَّ 
وَّ  إَّلْي َّوْمَّي ْن ِ وَّ إ َّكْي َّزَّ مِن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ ي َّزَّى صُّق ْزَّة ً إ َّوْ ذَّماً كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ن ِالص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّال   ق َّا ِن ْ ع َّلَّن َّهَّا إَّلْوَّج َّغُّ صَّل َّث ْ  
إ ِذ َّإ ن َّزَّإ َّب ْ .
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: 'The limit for the postnatal period of refraining from prayer 
has been set at eighteen days because the minimum duration of menstruation is three 
days, the maximum is ten days, and the average is five days.  
Thus, Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has designated the days of postnatal bleeding (nifas) to 
correspond with the minimum, average, and maximum days of menstruation.'  
[AL SADUQ] 
The reports that mention a forty-day period for refraining (from prayer during postnatal 
bleeding) and beyond until purification are considered weak (defective) and were 
narrated for purposes of precaution (taqiyya).  
Only those who differ from the mainstream position (i.e., opposing sects) give legal 
rulings based on such reports. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 389 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 210
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢١٠

hadiths.en:
Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} that He said: I asked 
him about a woman who experiences labor pains for one or two days or more and sees 
yellowish discharge or blood. What should She do regarding prayer?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'She should pray as long as She has not given birth. If the pain 
overwhelms her, She should pray when She recovers.'.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 392.

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 20, H. 211
reference.location: Volume 1 › GHUSL FOR MENSTRUATION AND POSTPARTUM
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٠، ح ٢١١

hadiths.ar:
213  -  وَّق َّال ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
«ق َّالَّ لَّه   
إَّلْحِمَّارُّ إ َّ ق َّلاَّ صَّن َّعْث َّ كَّذ َّإ ن ِي ن َّي ْهِ ن ِا َّصَّان ِعِهِ وَّ كَّق  َّي ْهِ 
إ ِجْذَّإهُّمَّا ن ِالْأ ُّج ْزَّى ي ُّم َّ لَّمْ ي ُّعِذْ ذ َّلِك َّ .  
 ق ا ذ إ ي ي مم إلزج ل للوص وء ص زب  ي ذي ه على إلأ رض  مزة  وإجذة  ي م ن ق ص هما و مسح ن هما ج ن ي ن ي ه وجاج ن ي ه ومسح على ط هز  
كق ي ه، وإ ذ إ كان  إلي ي مم للح ن ان ه  ص زب  ي ذي ه على إلأ رض  مزة  وإجذة ، ي م ن ق ص هما ومسح ن هما ج ن ي ن ي ه وجاج ن ي ه، ي م ص زب  
ي ذي ه على إلأ رض  مزة  إ ج زى ومسح على ط هز ي ذي ه ق وق  إلكف  ق لي لا وي ن ذإ  ن مسح إلي من ى ق ن ل إلي سزى.
hadiths.en:
From Zurarah, he asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}:  
'Would you not inform me from where you derived and said that wiping is done on part 
of head and part of feet?'  
Imam {a.s} smiled and said:  
'O’ Zurarah, it was stated by the Messenger of Allah {saws} and it was revealed in the Book 
of Allah {SWT}. Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, said: "So wash your faces" (Surah Al-Ma’idah 5:6). 
Thus, we understood that the entire face must be washed.  
Then Imam {a.s} said: "and your hands up to the elbows."  
Thus, Allah {SWT} has connected the hands up to the elbows with the face, so we 
understood that they must be washed up to the elbows.  
Then, Allah {SWT} separated between the statements and said: "and wipe your heads."  
Thus, we understood from "your heads" that wiping is done on part of the head due to 
the use of "bi" (preposition indicating partiality).  
Then, Allah {SWT} connected the feet to the head just as Allah {SWT} connected the hands 
to the face and said: "and your feet up to the ankles."  
Thus, we understood, by connecting them to the head, that wiping is done on part of 
them.' 
Imam {a.s} continued: 'The Messenger of Allah {saws} explained this to the people, but they 
neglected it.  
Then Allah {SWT} said: "If you do not find water, then seek clean earth and wipe over your 
faces and hands with it."  
Thus, when Allah {SWT} waived the requirement of wudu for one who does not find water, 
Allah {SWT} replaced some of the washing with wiping, as Allah {SWT} said, " with your 
faces."  
Then Allah {SWT} connected it with: " and your hands from it ," meaning from that 
tayammum.  
This is because Allah {SWT} knew that not all of the dust would reach the face, as some 
of it would adhere to some parts of the hand and not all of the hand.  
Then Allah {SWT} said: "Allah {SWT} does not intend to make difficulty for you," and difficulty 
means constraint.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 30 • Ilal al -Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 279 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 61 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 62 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 282 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol. 2, 
p. 256 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 289 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 596 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 212
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٢

hadiths.ar:
214  -  وسَّا َّلَّ عُّن َّي ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّج ْن َّث َّ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّح ِذِ إَّلْمَّاءَّ ق َّالَّ  
وَّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّمُّز ُّ ن ِالز َّكِي  َّه ِ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ مَّعَّهُّ ذَّلْو  ق َّال رَّب  َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ هُّوَّ رَّب  ُّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ق َّلْي َّي َّي َّم َّمْ  
وَّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّح ْن ِث ُّ وَّ مَّعَّهُّ ق َّذْرُّ مَّا ي َّكْق ِي هِ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ لِوُّص ُّوءِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إ َّ ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ ن ِالْمَّاءِ إ َّوْ  ي َّي َّي َّم َّمُّ ق َّال 
ي َّزَّى إ َّن  َّهُّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ج ُّعِلَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ن ِصْف ُّ إَّلْوُّص ُّوءِ .   
ومي ى إ صاب  إلمي ي مم إلماء ورج ا إ ن  ي ق ذر على ماء إ ج ز إ و ط ن  إ ن ه ي ق ذر علي ه كما إ رإذة ق عسز علي ه ذ لك ، ق ا ن  ن ط زة إ لى إلماء 
ي ن ق ض  ي ي ممه وعلي ه إ ن  ي عي ذ إلي ي مم، ق ا ن  إ صاب  إلماء وق ذ ذج ل ق ى  إلصلاة  ق لي ص زب  ولي ي وص ا  ما لم ي زكغ، ق ا ن  كان  ق ذ ركغ  
ق لي مض  ق ى  صلاي ه ق ان  إلي ي مم إ جذ إلطهو ري ن ، ومن  ي ي مم ي م إ صاب  إلماء ق علي ه إلع سل إ ن  كان  ج ن ن ا وإلوص وء إ ن  لم ي كن   
ج ن ن ا، ق ا ن  إ صاب  إلماء وق ذ صلى ن ي ي مم وهو ق ى  وق ث  ق ق ذ ي مث  صلاي ه ولأ إ عاذة  علي ه.
hadiths.en:
From Zurarah, he narrates that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, said:  
'One day, the Messenger of Allah {saws} said to Ammar while on a journey,  
"O’ Ammar, it has reached us that you became junub (ritually impure due to sexual 
discharge). What did you do?"  
Ammar replied: "I rolled in the dirt, O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}."  
The Prophet then said to him, "This is how a donkey rolls. You should have done this 
instead."  
Prophet {saws} then placed his hands on the clean earth, wiped his forehead with his 
fingers, and then wiped the back of each hand with the other hand, and he did not 
repeat it ’.  
[AL SADUQ] 
When a person performs tayammum for wudu (ablution), he should strike his hands 
on the earth once, shake them off, and then wipe his forehead and eyebrows, and then 
the backs of his hands.  
If the tayammum is for janabah (major ritual impurity), he should strike his hands on 
the earth once, shake them off, and wipe his forehead and eyebrows.  
Then, he strikes his hands on the earth again, wipes the backs of his hands up to a little 
above the wrists, and starts by wiping the right hand before the left.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 580 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 360 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 213
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٣

hadiths.ar:
215  - وَّق َّال ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ وَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ : ق ُّلْن َّا لِأ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ُّل  لَّمْ ي ُّصِث ْ مَّاءً وَّ جَّص َّزَّب ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ ق َّي َّي َّم َّمَّ 
وَّ صَّل َّى رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ ي ُّم َّ إ َّصَّاب َّ إَّلْمَّاءَّ إ َّ ي َّن ْق ُّض ُّ إَّلز َّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ إ َّوْ ي َّق ْطَّعُّهُّمَّا وَّ ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّصَّلِّى   ق َّال   ي َّمْص ِى  ق ِى   
صَّلاَّي ِهِ ق َّي ُّي ِم ُّهَّا وَّ لأَّ ي َّن ْق ُّص ُّهَّا لِمَّكَّان ِ إَّلْمَّاءِ لِأ َّن  َّهُّ ذَّج َّلَّهَّا وَّ هُّوَّ عَّلَّى طُّهْزٍ ن ِي َّي َّم ُّمٍ وَّق َّالَّ  ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ ذَّج َّلَّهَّا وَّ هُّوَّ مُّي َّي َّمِّم  
ق َّصَّل َّى رَّكْعَّه ً ي ُّم َّ إ َّجْذَّب َّ ق َّا َّصَّاب َّ مَّاءً ق َّال ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّن ْن ِى  عَّلَّى مَّا مَّص َّى مِن ْ صَّلاَّي ِهِ إَّل َّي ِى  صَّل َّى ن ِالي  َّي َّم ُّمِ .
hadiths.en:
Ubaidullah ibn Ali Al -Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
about a man who becomes junub (ritually impure due to sexual discharge) and does 
not find water.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He should perform tayammum with clean earth (sa’eed), and when he 
finds water, he should perform ghusl, but he does not need to repeat the prayer.'  
He also asked about a man who passes by a well but does not have a bucket with him. 
Imam {a.s} said: 'He is not required to enter the well because the Lord {AZJ} of the water is 
also the Lord {AZJ} of the earth, so he should perform tayammum.'.  
He asked further about a man who becomes junub and has only enough water to 
perform wudu (ablution) for prayer; should he use the water for wudu or perform 
tayammum?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'No, he should do tayammum. Do you not see that only half of wudu is 
needed of him?'  
[AL SADUQ] 
If a person who has performed tayammum finds water and hopes to find more water 
later or thinks he might find it as intended but then struggles to find it, merely seeing 
the water invalidates his tayammum, and he must repeat it.  
If he finds water after starting his prayer but has not yet bowed (Rukuh), he should 
interrupt and perform wudu. If he has already bowed, he should continue with his 
prayer, as tayammum is one of the forms of purification. If a person performs 
tayammum and later finds water, he must perform ghusl if he was in a state of janabah 
or wudu if he was not, but if he has already completed his prayer with tayammum 
during its time, then his prayer is valid and does not need to be repeated.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 214
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٤

hadiths.ar:
216   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّم َّارُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلي  َّي َّم ُّمِ مِن َّ إَّلْوُّص ُّوءِ وَّ مِن َّ إَّلْح َّن َّان َّه ِ وَّ مِن َّ 
إَّلْحَّي ْض ِ لِلن ِّسَّاءِ سَّوَّإء  ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah and Muhammad ibn Muslim said: We asked Abu Ja'far {a.s} about a man 
who does not find water, and the time for prayer arrives, so he performs tayammum 
and prays two rak'ahs. Then he finds water. Should he invalidate the two rak'ahs, does 
he interrupt them, perform wudu, and then repeat the prayer?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'No, he should continue his prayer and complete it, and the presence 
of water does not invalidate it because he entered into it while in a state of purity 
through tayammum.' 
Zurarah also said: I asked him about a person who entered the prayer with tayammum, 
prayed one rak'ah, then broke his wudu and now has also found water.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He should go out, perform wudu, and then continue from where he left 
off in the prayer that he began with tayammum.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 205 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
167 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 49 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 562 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 215
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٥

hadiths.en:
Ammar ibn Musa al -Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
about tayammum for wudu (ablution), janabah (major ritual impurity), and 
menstruation for women.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'Yes, it is the same.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 162 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 1, p. 212 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 583 • Tafsir al-Safi, Part 1, p. 456 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 315 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 362 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 216
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٦

hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} about a 
man who has sores and wounds and becomes junub (ritually impure due to sexual 
discharge). 
Imam {a.s} said: 'There is no harm if he performs tayammum and does not make ghusl 
(ritual bath).' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 217
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٧

hadiths.ar:
219  -  وَّ ق ِي لَّ لِزَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ إ ِن  َّ ق ُّلاَّن اً إ َّصَّان َّي ْه ج َّن َّان َّه   وَّ هُّوَّ مَّح ْذُّور  ق َّع َّس َّلُّوةُّ ق َّمَّاب َّ  
ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
'One who is suffering from a severe stomach illness or has broken bones should 
perform tayammum and should not perform ghusl (ritual bathing).'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 348 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 218
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٨

hadiths.ar:
220  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :   عَّن ْ مَّح ْذُّورٍ إ َّصَّان َّي ْهُّ ج َّن َّان َّه   ق َّق َّال 
إِجْي َّلَّمَّ ق َّلْي َّي َّي َّم َّمْ .  
وإلح ن ث  إ ذ إ ج اف  على ن ق سه من  إلن زذ ي ي مم.
hadiths.en:
It was said to the Messenger of Allah {saws}:  
'O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, so-and-so became junub (ritually impure due to sexual 
discharge) and had smallpox, so they washed him and he died.'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'They killed him! Why did they not ask? Why did they not perform 
tayammum for him? Surely, the cure for ignorance is asking questions.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Sarayer, Part 3, p. 612 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 154 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 219
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢١٩

hadiths.ar:
221   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّي ْسَّزَّة :   عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  إَّلس َّق َّزِ ق َّلاَّ ي َّح ِذُّ إَّلْمَّاءَّ ق َّي َّي َّي َّم َّمُّ وَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ي ُّم َّ ي َّا ْي ِى  عَّلَّى إَّلْمَّاءِ  
وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ س َّى ْء  مِن َّ إَّلْوَّق ْث ِ إ َّ ي َّمْص ِى  عَّلَّى صَّلاَّي ِهِ إ َّمْ ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ وَّ ي ُّعِي ذُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ق َّال لاَّي ِهِ ق َّا ِ ن  َّ رَّب  َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ  
هُّوَّ رَّب  ُّ إَّلي  ُّزَّإب ِ .
hadiths.en:
It was asked of Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} was asked about a man 
with smallpox who became junub (ritually impure due to sexual discharge).  
Imam {a.s} said: 'If he became junub (through intercourse), he should perform ghusl, but 
if he had a nocturnal emission (i.e., he experienced it while sleeping), he should 
perform tayammum.'. 
[AL SADUQ] 
And if a person in a state of janabah fears harm from the cold, they may perform 
tayammum. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Vol 3, p. 68 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 198 • 
Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 162 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 46 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 552 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 220
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢١، ح ٢٢٠
